cards.question: How many times a day should you brush your teeth?
cards.answer: 2 times a day — once in the morning and once before bed!

cards.question: How long should you brush your teeth?
cards.answer: For 2 whole minutes — that's as long as a short song!

cards.question: How often should you change your toothbrush?
cards.answer: Every 3 months, or sooner if the bristles look worn out!

cards.question: Should you brush your tongue too?
cards.answer: Yes! Brushing your tongue removes bad breath bacteria!

cards.question: What kind of toothbrush is best for kids?
cards.answer: A soft-bristled toothbrush that fits comfortably in your mouth!

cards.question: How often should you floss your teeth?
cards.answer: Once a day — usually before bedtime is best!

cards.question: Why is flossing important?
cards.answer: It removes food and plaque from between your teeth where your toothbrush can't reach!

cards.question: At what age should kids start flossing?
cards.answer: As soon as two teeth touch each other — usually around age 2 or 3!

cards.question: How many baby teeth do kids have?
cards.answer: 20 baby teeth in total — 10 on top and 10 on the bottom!

cards.question: How many adult teeth will you grow?
cards.answer: 32 adult teeth, including 4 wisdom teeth!

cards.question: What is the hardest substance in your body?
cards.answer: Tooth enamel — the outer layer of your teeth!

cards.question: What causes cavities?
cards.answer: Bacteria eat leftover sugar on your teeth and make acids that create holes called cavities!

cards.question: What are molars used for?
cards.answer: Molars are the big flat teeth in the back — they grind and crush food!

cards.question: Which drink is best for your teeth?
cards.answer: Water! It washes away food and helps keep your mouth clean!

cards.question: Which foods help make teeth strong?
cards.answer: Milk, cheese, yogurt, and vegetables — they have calcium and vitamins!

cards.question: Why is candy bad for your teeth?
cards.answer: Sugar feeds bacteria in your mouth, which then makes acids that damage your teeth!

cards.question: Is juice good or bad for teeth?
cards.answer: Juice has a lot of sugar — it's better to eat the fruit instead!

cards.question: How often should you visit the dentist?
cards.answer: Every 6 months — twice a year for a checkup and cleaning!

cards.question: When should a child have their first dentist visit?
cards.answer: By their first birthday or when their first tooth appears!

cards.question: What does fluoride do for your teeth?
cards.answer: Fluoride makes your tooth enamel stronger and helps prevent cavities!
